title: rich molten energizing malaysian chocolate pasta
description: a decadent, sweet, and energizing dessert dish that combines the richness of dark chocolate with the creaminess of pasta water emulsion, featuring notes of Malaysian spices and satvik-friendly ingredients.
instructions: combine the dark chocolate, ghee, and honey in a double boiler and melt over low heat.
whisk in the pasta water, coconut cream, vanilla extract, cinnamon powder, cardamom powder, and black pepper until smooth.
remove from heat and stir in the chai spice blend and dried coconut flakes.
pour the mixture into a serving dish and allow to set at room temperature for at least 2 hours.
once set, use a kitchen torch to caramelize the top of the pasta, if desired.
serve immediately, garnished with additional dried coconut flakes and a sprinkle of cinnamon powder.
note: for an extra creamy texture, refrigerate the pasta for 30 minutes before serving and then return to room temperature before serving.
for a more intense chocolate flavor, use a higher percentage of cocoa solids in the dark chocolate.
for a lighter texture, substitute some of the coconut cream with heavy cream or whole milk.
